touch.title: Physical Touch
touch.desc: You communicate love through closeness — a hand on the back, a long hug, sitting pressed together on the couch. Words can lie. Touch doesn't. For you, presence is physical.
touch.advice: Not everyone expresses love this way. Tell people what you need — they can't read your body if they speak a different love language.
youProbably: Hold people a beat longer than expected
Reach for someone's hand without thinking
Feel most hurt when someone flinches away
Judge relationships by how much physical warmth exists

words.title: Words of Affirmation
words.tagline: you hear love before you feel it
words.desc: A well-timed "I'm proud of you" can carry you for weeks. You remember compliments word-for-word, and silence from someone you love feels deafening.
words.advice: You give what you need — so you probably over-compliment and under-ask. Say it: "I need to hear that you value me."
youProbably: Screenshot meaningful texts
Remember exact words of a compliment from years ago
Feel crushed by a passive-aggressive tone
Write long heartfelt messages at 2am

acts.title: Acts of Service
acts.tagline: love is a verb, not a word
acts.desc: You don't want to hear "I love you" — you want to see it. A filled gas tank, a meal when you're tired, a solved problem you didn't ask for help with. That's romance.
acts.advice: Be careful not to over-give and resent the imbalance. Notice when someone serves you in their own language, even if it's not yours.
youProbably: Do things for people without being asked
Feel deeply loved when someone handles your stress
Get frustrated by empty promises
Show love by fixing, carrying, handling logistics

gifts.title: Receiving Gifts
gifts.tagline: it's the thought that proves the love
gifts.desc: It was never about the price tag. It's the proof that someone thought of you when you weren't there. A random snack, a playlist, a "saw this and thought of you" — that's everything.
gifts.advice: People might misread this as materialism. Help them understand: the gift is evidence of attention, not transaction.
youProbably: Keep small meaningful objects forever
Feel hurt when someone forgets an occasion
Notice when someone remembers a tiny thing you mentioned
Put thought into every gift you give

time.title: Quality Time
time.tagline: your love currency is undivided attention
time.desc: A phone on the table during dinner is a rejection. You don't need activities — you need someone fully present, with nowhere else to be and no one else to text.
time.advice: In a distracted world, this is the hardest language to have. Be explicit: "I need your eyes, not your schedule."
youProbably: Notice immediately when someone checks their phone mid-conversation
Treasure lazy mornings with no plans
Feel rejected by chronic busyness
Fall hardest for people who make time stop
